title: D3.2 – Water Extraction and Liquefaction Design Report
author: Kiewiet, Luca
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The WECS (Water Extraction, Capturing and Liquefaction Subsystem) design will be split into two sections: the extraction and the capturing and liquefaction subsystem. For the other subsystems, they will be considered as one system. A schematic overview of the WECS and its design inside the Thermal Vacuum Chamber (TVAC) (and partially outside) is presented in Figure 1-1.  
  As a recap, the extraction subsystem its functions are to:  
  •   be filled with icy-regolith simulant in the crucible,  
  •   sublimate the ice in the icy-regolith simulant,  
  •   allow water vapour to flow towards to the capturing subsystem,  
  •   empty dry regolith from the crucible. The capturing and liquefaction subsystem’s functions are to:  
  •   capture the water vapour,  
  •   transfer liquid water to the storage container.
steps_raw:
The WECS (Water Extraction, Capturing and Liquefaction Subsystem) design will be split into two sections: the extraction and the capturing and liquefaction subsystem. For the other subsystems, they will be considered as one system. A schematic overview of the WECS and its design inside the Thermal Vacuum Chamber (TVAC) (and partially outside) is presented in Figure 1-1.  
  As a recap, the extraction subsystem its functions are to:  
  •   be filled with icy-regolith simulant in the crucible,  
  •   sublimate the ice in the icy-regolith simulant,  
  •   allow water vapour to flow towards to the capturing subsystem,  
  •   empty dry regolith from the crucible. The capturing and liquefaction subsystem’s functions are to:  
  •   capture the water vapour,  
  •   transfer liquid water to the storage container.
